mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor income expansion that supports aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy heightens recession risk and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and a rising unemployment rate jointly signal deteriorating real output and labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL signal persistent inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain higher rates for longer, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 45 percent of observations during tightening cycles when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate above 4 percent and declining retail sales creates a credit-constrained demand collapse that produces sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration and collapsing household income expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
